name: Traffic Flow
description: A network simulation where vehicles, agents, or abstract traffic units choose routes, occupy lanes, respond to signals, and create congestion or throughput effects.
design_notes: Traffic flow matters when players can see how roads, signals, demand, accidents, weather, and population pressure create congestion. The implementation should separate aggregate flow from visible vehicles, define pathing constraints, pooling, update order, and debug overlays for bottlenecks.
design_purpose: Create readable cause and effect between network layout and movement outcomes.
Support city, base, factory, or open-world simulation with scalable vehicle behavior.
Give players feedback about congestion, capacity, route choices, and infrastructure upgrades.
player_fantasy: The player feels the transport network breathe as traffic responds to their layout and management decisions.

parameters.name: traffic_network_id
parameters.description: Network graph containing route segments and intersections.

parameters.name: vehicle_agent_id
parameters.description: Visible or abstract unit moving through the traffic network.

parameters.name: route_segment_id
parameters.description: Current network segment used for occupancy and congestion.

parameters.name: traffic_demand_profile_id
parameters.description: Demand profile that creates route requests for agents.

parameters.name: congestion_index
parameters.description: Normalized occupancy pressure for a segment or region.

parameters.name: signal_phase_id
parameters.description: Intersection control state affecting route movement.

parameters.name: despawn_policy_id
parameters.description: Rule for removing visible vehicles from the world.

parameters.name: simulation_tick_rate
parameters.description: Update cadence for route, lane, and signal decisions.

edge_cases: A route becomes invalid after the player deletes a road or changes direction rules.
Visible vehicles reach a blocked intersection while aggregate flow still assumes capacity.
An emergency or scripted vehicle needs priority without corrupting normal traffic order.
Weather changes friction or visibility while vehicles are midway through intersections.
A save occurs with vehicles inside temporary construction or detour segments.
common_bugs: Vehicle agents choose paths before updated road ownership or lane closures are committed.
Despawning offscreen vehicles loses passengers, cargo, or delivery state.
Traffic signals update after vehicles move and allow impossible intersection overlaps.
Congestion overlays read visual vehicle count but ignore abstract queued demand.
Pathfinding reroutes every tick and consumes excessive CPU on large maps.
balancing_notes: Traffic should expose bottlenecks early enough for players to diagnose them.
Fully realistic traffic can be less playable than a model with clear capacity and routing rules.
Abstract agents improve scale but need visible samples and overlays to maintain trust.
accessibility_notes: Provide colorblind-safe congestion overlays with numeric or patterned alternatives.
Let players slow or pause the simulation while inspecting complex intersections.
Use clear icons and filters for road classes, signal states, and route failures.

implementation_notes.unity: Represent the traffic network as graph data and use pooled vehicle agents for visible samples. Process signal phases, route movement, despawn folding, and congestion metrics in deterministic order, then draw debug overlays for route_segment_id pressure.
implementation_notes.godot: Use graph resources for roads and a simulation node for tick updates. Keep visible VehicleBody or CharacterBody nodes as projections of route state, and fold them back into aggregate demand through despawn_policy_id.
implementation_notes.unreal: Mass Entity, StateTree, or custom systems can scale vehicle agents while Actors represent nearby traffic. Run signal and route updates from an authoritative subsystem and replicate aggregate congestion or visible agents as needed.
implementation_notes.web: Store traffic graph state in arrays keyed by route_segment_id and update with a fixed tick. Render only nearby agents in Canvas, Phaser, or Three.js, and keep pathing caches invalidated by explicit network revision ids.

relationships.reason: Traffic flow can use local steering and flocking rules for density and lane behavior.
Vehicle controls and traffic simulation need collision and authority boundaries when player vehicles enter traffic.
Population density can feed traffic demand and spawn budgets.

example_games.title: Cities: Skylines
example_games.note: Uses traffic flow as a major city planning system.

example_games.title: OpenTTD
example_games.note: Uses transport networks and route throughput as central play.
